title: tests: Add tests
body:
After adding these two tests, all commands have corresponding test files.

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body:
:pushpin: Commit 7614843 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 76148431a843ac83e95ed8e905af631bc7c9e2ee -->

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/a52e99c3c8c5b61a50edae9377c867c2cd68b968.1528816478511322368/index.html)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing a52e99c3c8c5b61a50edae9377c867c2cd68b968 to master...

@TomSweeneyRedHat I remember putting that there because it was jamming everything into one line. It was not returning a new line after each line. I just tested it without that and I am still seeing the same thing.
With the check:
```
➜  buildah git:(cache) ✗ sudo buildah images --format "{{.ID}}"
cc510acfcd701a409014118d5f417f0022520802a26c650866b8a9594d75f3a7
3fd9065eaf02feaf94d68376da52541925650b81698c53c6824d92ff63f98353
```
Without the check:
```
➜  buildah git:(cache) ✗ sudo buildah images --format "{{.ID}}"
cc510acfcd701a409014118d5f417f0022520802a26c650866b8a9594d75f3a73fd9065eaf02feaf94d68376da52541925650b81698c53c6824d92ff63f98353%
```

I use zshell, so could be because of that, not sure...
Sounds familiar.  Is there a way to only do this if when there is multiple lines of output?
Created #782 to hopefully address the one line return for the formats test in bats.
